signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: Trump's announcement of 25% tariffs on European autos sets up immediate downside pressure on EUR/USD toward the 1.1600 handle. This trade protectionism reduces European export competitiveness and reinforces the EUR COT net short of -18,293 contracts. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) remains a hold, providing no hawkish offset to the trade-driven weakness.

signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Intervention Risk
signals.text: JPY's 1W momentum (+1.08%) runs against the 300bp US-JPY yield gap, but confirmed $34.5 billion in yen intervention creates a floor for JPY strength. The JPY COT net short of -88,543 (30.6% long) presents a massive short-squeeze risk if USD/JPY breaks below recent support. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) is the catalyst for resolving whether the 0.75% rate will be hiked to further compress the carry advantage.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD COT net long +48,299 (74.8% long) creates a significant crowded-long unwind risk despite the +35bp carry advantage over USD. While the RBA maintains a hike bias at 4.10%, the extreme retail short positioning (90% short) suggests a contrarian risk that could exacerbate a move if the 0.7200 level fails. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 0% hike priced) on 5 May determines if the hawkish bias can sustain the current bid.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Price Compression
signals.text: WTI Crude's 2.98% drop to $101.9 on US-Iran peace talk hopes weakens the CAD's fundamental support, targeting a move back toward 1.3650. The CAD COT net short of -54,703 reinforces the bearish bias as the 150bp carry advantage for USD persists. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) serves as the resolution point for the current neutral policy stance.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning Overstretch
signals.text: GBP COT net long +32,637 (67.3% long) suggests the 1W rally of +0.81% is reaching a point of exhaustion near 1.3600. The BoE's active hold signals June risks, which limits further upside as the carry spread remains neutral at 0bp. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is the catalyst for any shift in the current hold bias.
